title: Promoted to Director
description: After 8 years with the company, finally made it to the leadership team. Leading a team of 15 talented people.
notes: It's surreal to think about how far I've come. The imposter syndrome is real, but I'm ready for this challenge.

title: Emma's first day of school
description: Watched Emma walk into kindergarten. She was so brave, I was the one crying in the parking lot.
notes: Time really does fly. Feels like yesterday she was learning to walk.

title: Ran first marathon
description: Completed the Chicago Marathon in 4:12. Never thought I'd be a runner, but here we are.
notes: Training for this taught me more about persistence than anything else in my life. The last 6 miles were pure mental strength.

title: Became a parent
description: Emma arrived at 3:42am. 7 lbs 3 oz. Our lives changed forever in the most beautiful way.
notes: Nothing prepares you for this moment. The love is overwhelming and terrifying at the same time.

title: Got married
description: Married my best friend in a small ceremony surrounded by the people we love most.
notes: The rain cleared just in time for the outdoor ceremony. It felt like everything aligned perfectly.

title: Bought first house
description: Signed the papers on a little craftsman in the suburbs. It needs work, but it's ours.
notes: The American dream feels different than I imagined, but standing in our own living room for the first time was magical.

title: Graduated from college
description: Finished my degree in Business Administration. Four years of late nights and early mornings finally paid off.
notes: Mom and Dad drove 6 hours to be there. Seeing their faces in the crowd made it all worth it.

title: Summer in Europe
description: Backpacked through 8 countries with my college roommate. Best summer of my life.
notes: We got lost in Prague, missed our train in Paris, and ate the best pasta in a tiny Roman alley. Pure freedom.

title: High school graduation
description: Graduated with honors. Voted 'Most Likely to Succeed' by my classmates.
notes: I had no idea what I wanted to do with my life, but I was ready to figure it out.
